mechanism_narrative: Accelerating retail sales reflected in RSAFS growth indicate persistent consumer spending strength that directly lifts corporate revenues and earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns following month-over-month RSAFS growth in the top quartile fail to outperform the bottom quartile by at least 1 percentage point annualized over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A three-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals rapid labor market deterioration that reduces household income and consumption, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50 percent of instances when UNRATE rises more than 0.3 percentage points over three months during any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in the federal funds rate and consumer price index tighten financial conditions and erode real corporate profit margins through higher discount rates and input costs, jointly generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal from FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL achieves a Rank IC below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are not significantly more negative than the unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth during post-recession recovery periods reflects rising capacity utilization and operating leverage that amplifies earnings surprises, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50 percent of post-recession recovery months identified by consecutive month-over-month INDPRO increases following a GDP contraction.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth above its trailing two-year median validates aggregate demand durability and supports upward earnings revisions, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following above-median GDPC1 growth do not exceed returns following below-median growth by at least 2 percent annualized over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate exceeding 4.5 percent restricts credit availability to corporations and households while raising equity discount rates, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 5-day returns in more than 55 percent of trading weeks when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5 percent during any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in housing starts and payroll employment confirm broad-based strength in residential investment and labor income, interacting to drive positive S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between HOUST and PAYEMS delivers a Rank IC below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns do not exceed single-proxy model returns (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth during early-cycle recovery signals accelerating labor income and pent-up demand release, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50 percent of early-cycle recovery periods defined by PAYEMS growth exceeding its 12-month trailing average after a GDP contraction.
